_comment: Committed, always-on. The 'deny' list is the hard safety backstop (enforced even under bypassPermissions). The 'allow' list reduces prompts when running in a prompting mode (default/plan). Unattended auto-run lives in the gitignored .claude/settings.local.json (defaultMode: bypassPermissions) so it stays on Brian's machine only. The 'python main.py' deny entries turn smoke-verifier's prose 'never launch the real server' rule into a deterministic guard (the real server binds :8080/:4000 and mutates the live sw_mush.db, colliding with a running instance or the parallel dev session); the in-process smoke harness boots no listeners so tests are unaffected. NOTE for Brian: this blocks an interactive 'python main.py' launch from INSIDE a Claude Code session (your documented restart path) — launch the server from a plain terminal outside Claude Code, or temporarily remove the entry. PowerShell read-only allow entries mirror the already-trusted Bash set for shell parity on this Windows box.
_hooks_comment: Deterministic enforcement layer for unattended runs (active in ALL permission modes, incl. bypassPermissions). PreToolUse world_yaml_additive_guard.py hard-blocks (exit 2) any Edit/Write/MultiEdit that would net-delete lines from data/worlds/clone_wars/{planets,maps}/*.yaml (map-safety invariant). PostToolUse post_edit_validate.py py_compiles edited *.py and yaml.safe_loads edited *.yaml, surfacing syntax errors back to Claude (exit 2). Command prefers venv python (has PyYAML), falls back to PATH python; ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.} resolves absolute at hook runtime or relative to cwd. Hooks reload on settings change / session start. Scripts are committed under .claude/hooks/.
